title: bertolonia scabra
intro: Bertolonia scabra, also known as the "scrub bertolonia" or "hairy bertolonia", is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, native to the tropical rainforests of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include the potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Bertolonia scabra is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves or roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
